chunks.en: Before I leave home, the first thing I always grab is my company ID badge.
chunks.ja: 家を出る前に、私がいつも最初に手に取るのは会社のIDバッジです。

chunks.en: The way I check I didn't forget anything is patting my pockets for my phone and keys.
chunks.ja: 忘れ物がないか確認する私のやり方は、ポケットを叩いてスマホと鍵を確認することです。

chunks.en: When I'm running late, I rush out the door and skip breakfast altogether.
chunks.ja: 遅刻しそうなときは、慌ててドアから飛び出して、朝食を完全に抜いてしまいます。

chunks.en: The one thing I often leave behind is my umbrella, even when it's clearly going to rain.
chunks.ja: 私がよく置き忘れるものは傘で、明らかに雨が降りそうなときでもです。

outline: Opening: my morning routine before heading out
Point 1: what I grab and how I check for forgotten things
Point 2: rushing out when I'm late
Wrap-up: the item I always forget

modelTalk.text: Right, so before I head out, there are a few things I always grab. My phone, my keys, and my wallet — those three are non-negotiable. I usually pat my pockets to double-check I've got everything. It's a silly little habit, but it works for me. If I'm not sure, I'll open my bag and take a quick look inside. When I'm running late, though, that whole routine goes out the window. I just grab whatever's closest and dash out the door. Then halfway down the street, I start panicking about what I've forgotten. Honestly, the thing I leave behind most often is my umbrella. I set it by the door, and then I completely blank on it. If it starts raining later, I always end up kicking myself. So these days I try to slow down, even when I'm in a rush. It saves me so much hassle later on.

chunks.en: Before I head out the door, the first thing I always grab is my phone and wallet.
chunks.ja: 家を出る前に、私がいつも最初に手に取るのはスマホと財布です。

chunks.en: The way I check I haven't forgotten anything is to pat down all my pockets quickly.
chunks.ja: 忘れ物がないか確認する私のやり方は、全部のポケットをさっと叩いて触ることです。

chunks.en: When I'm running late, I tend to grab my bag and rush out without double-checking.
chunks.ja: 遅刻しそうなときは、確認もせずにカバンをつかんで慌てて飛び出してしまいがちです。

chunks.en: The one thing I often leave behind is my umbrella, especially when it's not raining yet.
chunks.ja: 私がよく置き忘れるものは傘で、特にまだ雨が降っていないときに忘れます。

outline: Opening: what I always grab before leaving
How I check for forgotten items
Wrap-up: the thing I often forget

modelTalk.text:
Right, so before I head out the door, there's always this little routine I go through. First things first, I grab my phone, my wallet and my keys — that's the holy trinity, honestly. I usually pat my pockets to double-check I've got everything on me. If something feels off, I'll stop and mentally run through my day. Do I need my laptop? An umbrella? That sort of thing.

Now, when I'm running late, it all goes out the window. I'm dashing around, half-dressed, shoving stuff into my bag. To be honest, that's exactly when I make mistakes.

The one thing I forever leave behind? My water bottle. I fill it up, set it on the counter, and then just... walk off without it. Every single time. That said, I'm trying to build a better habit. These days I leave everything by the door the night before. It genuinely saves me a mad scramble in the morning.
